eras.name: The Birth of the Transistor
eras.description: The invention of the transistor at Bell Labs marked the beginning of the semiconductor age. This revolutionary device would eventually replace vacuum tubes and enable the miniaturization of electronic devices.
eras.impact: The transistor invention laid the foundation for all modern electronics and commenced the rapid advancement of semiconductor technology.

eras.name: Integrated Circuit Era
eras.description: Jack Kilby at Texas Instruments demonstrated the first integrated circuit, while Robert Noyce at Fairchild developed a more practical silicon-based version.

companies.name: Fairchild Semiconductor
companies.contribution: Developed the planar process and silicon-based ICs

eras.impact: Integrated circuits enabled the creation of more complex electronic devices while reducing size and cost.

eras.name: Microprocessor Revolution
eras.description: Intel introduces the 4004, the first commercial microprocessor, starting the microcomputer revolution.
eras.impact: The microprocessor made personal computing possible and established the foundation for the digital revolution.
